title: Script Object
description: Object that represents a script or add-in.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: edit
methods.description: Invokes the default edit behavior for this script or add-in.

methods.name: run
methods.description: Runs this script or add-in, if it's not already running.

methods.name: stop
methods.description: If this script or add-in is running, this method will stop it. The isRunning property can be used to determine if it is running. If the script or add-in is not running and this method is called, there is no effect.

methods.name: unlink
methods.description: Unlinks this script or add-in. This removes it from Fusion's list of linked scripts and add-ins, so it is no longer displayed in the dialog, and if it's an add-in, it will no longer run on startup.   This is only valid for a script or add-in that is linked; the sourceLocation property returns LinkedScriptSourceLocation.

properties.name: appStoreURL
properties.description: For an add-in installed from the Autodesk App Store, this property returns the URL on the store for the page of this app. This property returns an empty string for all scripts and add-ins not installed from the App Store and if there is a problem determining the URL for an App Store app.

properties.name: author
properties.description: Returns the author information associated with this script or add-in.

properties.name: description
properties.description: Gets the description of this script or add-in.

properties.name: folder
properties.description: Gets the full path of the folder that contains this script or add-in.

properties.name: helpFilename
properties.description: Returns the filename of a local html file that serves as the help file for this script or add-in. This filename is defined in the manifest of the script or add-in using the "helpFilename" setting.

properties.name: iconFilename
properties.description: Returns the filename of the image file that can be used as the icon for this script or add-in. This filename is defined in the manifest of the script or add-in using the "iconFilename" setting.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Gets the ID of this script or add-in. This is typically a GUID and is assumed to be unique with respect to all other add-ins.

properties.name: isAddIn
properties.description: Gets if this Script object represents a script or an add-in. Returns true if it is an add-in.

properties.name: isEditable
properties.description: Indicates if this script or add-in is blocked from being edited by the user in the "Scripts and Add-Ins" dialog.

properties.name: isFavorite
properties.description: Gets and sets whether this script or add-in is a favorite of the user.

properties.name: isInternal
properties.description: Indicates if this is an internal script or add-in that is delivered with Fusion. Returns true if it is an internal script or add-in.

properties.name: isRunning
properties.description: Gets if this script or add-in is currently running.

properties.name: isRunOnStartup
properties.description: Gets and sets whether this add-in will automatically run when Fusion is started. This property is only valid when the isAddIn property returns true.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets whether the script or add-in is visible within the “Scripts and Add-Ins” dialog. By default, all scripts and add-ins are visible. Setting this to false will cause it to be hidden and unloaded if it is already running. Also, if it’s an add-in set to load on startup, it will no longer be loaded.

properties.name: location
properties.description: Gets which standard location this script is located.

properties.name: manifestContent
properties.description: Gets the full contents of the manifest file associated with this script or add-in. This is particularly useful if you have any custom information defined in the manifest. The manifest file uses JSON to format its content.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets the name of this script or add-in.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: programmingLanguage
properties.description: Returns the programming language this script or add-in is written in.

properties.name: targetOperatingSystem
properties.description: Returns the operating systems this script or add-in is available for.

properties.name: version
properties.description: Returns the version information associated with this script or add-in.

appStoreURL.title: Script.appStoreURL Property
appStoreURL.description: For an add-in installed from the Autodesk App Store, this property returns the URL on the store for the page of this app. This property returns an empty string for all scripts and add-ins not installed from the App Store and if there is a problem determining the URL for an App Store app.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = script_var.appStoreURL

classType.title: Script.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.Script.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::Script::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

edit.title: Script.edit Method
edit.description: Invokes the default edit behavior for this script or add-in.

syntax.python: returnValue = script_var.edit()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = script_var->edit();

run.title: Script.run Method
run.description: Runs this script or add-in, if it's not already running.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = script_var.run()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = script_var.run(waitForFinish)

parameters.name: waitForFinish
parameters.description: Specifies if call will wait until the script or add-in has finished running. For add-ins, this should always be false, because they typically continue to run for the entire Fusion session.   For scripts, there are cases where you might want to set this to true, where you need to wait for the script to finish because you want to do something with whatever it creates. Typically, this should be false, so it starts the script and immediately returns.   This is an optional argument whose default value is False.

stop.title: Script.stop Method
stop.description: If this script or add-in is running, this method will stop it. The isRunning property can be used to determine if it is running. If the script or add-in is not running and this method is called, there is no effect.

syntax.python: returnValue = script_var.stop()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = script_var->stop();

unlink.title: Script.unlink Method
unlink.description: Unlinks this script or add-in. This removes it from Fusion's list of linked scripts and add-ins, so it is no longer displayed in the dialog, and if it's an add-in, it will no longer run on startup. This is only valid for a script or add-in that is linked; the sourceLocation property returns LinkedScriptSourceLocation.

syntax.python: returnValue = script_var.unlink()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = script_var->unlink();

unlink.return_description: Returns true if the unlink was successful.
